text: Patient has colon cancer that began as recurrent polyps and has grown across a stretch of the colon
transcript_quote: It started with the polyps — for years they kept finding them, taking them out, finding more back there. Then last year it was not a polyp anymore. The cancer has grown across a whole stretch of the colon now.

text: Oncology specialist advised further cancer treatment would harm more than help; patient elected to stop disease-directed treatment
transcript_quote: He said anything more they could do would take more out of me than it would take out of the cancer. And I said, then I believe we are done.

text: Patient's goals of care: remain at home, comfort-focused, avoid hospital death
transcript_quote: Staying right here. This house, my flower beds out front, my shows, Karen, and the cat. I do not want to die in a hospital hallway under those lights. And I do not want to hurt more than I have to.

text: Patient reports dull, low abdominal pain most days that comes and goes
transcript_quote: The belly aches most days — dull, low down, comes and goes.

text: Patient reports chronic back pain present for 20 years
transcript_quote: my back — the back has been bad for twenty years, that is nothing new

text: Pain is currently bearable/controlled on current medications
transcript_quote: The pills keep it just about bearable.

text: Patient takes acetaminophen 325 mg
transcript_quote: Acetaminophen, the 325s.

text: Patient takes simvastatin 10 mg for cholesterol
transcript_quote: Simvastatin, 10 milligrams, for cholesterol.

text: Patient takes oxycodone extended-release (OxyContin) 10 mg
transcript_quote: The OxyContin — oxycodone 10 milligram extended-release.

text: Patient takes Percocet (acetaminophen 325 mg / oxycodone 10 mg) for bad pain stretches
transcript_quote: Percocet — that is acetaminophen 325 with oxycodone 10 — for the bad stretches.

text: Patient takes tramadol 50 mg
transcript_quote: Tramadol 50.

text: Patient takes naproxen sodium 220 mg
transcript_quote: Naproxen sodium, the 220s.

text: Patient takes hydrocodone extended-release 10 mg capsule
transcript_quote: A hydrocodone 10 milligram extended-release capsule.

text: Patient has leftover amoxicillin-clavulanate from the dentist
transcript_quote: An amoxicillin-clavulanate left over from the dentist.

text: Patient uses a fluoride gel
transcript_quote: And a fluoride gel.

text: Nurse counseled patient/family that medication list will be organized into a written schedule identifying each medicine's purpose and breakthrough pain med
transcript_quote: Over our next visits we will get it organized — a written schedule, what each medicine is for, which one is for breakthrough pain

text: Bowel function screening: patient reports bowels slower than before but moving most days
transcript_quote: Slower than I used to, but yes, most days. Karen keeps pushing the prune juice.

text: Counseled on constipation prevention with opioids: continue prune juice, fluids, and report if bowels stop
transcript_quote: Keep pushing it, Karen — fluids, and you tell me right away if things stop. With this cancer and these medicines, that is the one we never ignore.

text: Patient reports poor/small appetite
transcript_quote: Appetite is small. I eat like a bird and Karen fusses.

text: Patient reports fatigue with increased daytime sleeping
transcript_quote: And I am tired. I sleep more afternoons than not.

text: Patient has history of anemia, possibly contributing to fatigue
transcript_quote: They always said my blood ran low — the anemia — so maybe that is some of it.

text: Two-physician hospice certification completed with the attending physician
transcript_quote: I have completed that certification with her attending doctor. That is done.

text: Formal notifications sent to primary physician and insurance regarding hospice coordination
transcript_quote: We also send the formal notifications — her primary doctor and her insurance both get notice that hospice is coordinating everything now.

text: Initial patient assessment / head-to-toe exam performed at this visit
transcript_quote: today counts as your initial assessment — I will look you over head to toe in a few minutes

text: Individualized plan of care developed reflecting patient's wishes (home, comfort, flowers, cat)

slots.procedure: development of individualized plan of care

transcript_quote: from it we build your individualized plan of care. That is the document that says what Nola wants, what we do about it, and who comes when.

text: Hospice care regime initiated
transcript_quote: our job is simple: comfort, at home, on your terms. That is exactly what hospice is for.

text: Patient declined a hospital bed at this time
transcript_quote: No hospital bed yet. I have slept in my own bed for forty years and I intend to keep at it a while.

text: Patient accepted installation of grab bars in the bathroom
transcript_quote: The grab bars I would take.

text: Care team to provide nursing visits a couple times weekly initially, with aide for bathing/dressing, social worker and chaplain available

slots.services: nursing visits, home health aide, social worker, chaplain

transcript_quote: I will come a couple of times a week to start, more whenever you need me. An aide can come to help with bathing and dressing... There is a social worker, and a chaplain if you ever want one.

text: 24/7 hospice on-call number provided to patient and family
transcript_quote: this number here answers twenty-four hours a day, every day of the year... You call, a hospice nurse answers.

text: Goals-of-care discussion: patient wishes no resuscitation, no CPR, no machines (DNR)
transcript_quote: Let me go easy. No machines, no pounding on my chest.

text: Patient wishes to avoid ambulance transport and emergency room unless she requests it
transcript_quote: What we will not do is put her through an ambulance ride and an emergency room, unless she asks for that.

text: Nurse follow-up visit scheduled in two days
transcript_quote: Dana will see you again in two days

text: Patient's niece Karen is primary family caregiver, staying most nights and managing paperwork
transcript_quote: I am Karen, her niece. I have been staying over most nights lately.

text: History and physical note documented for the encounter
Patient's pain is currently well controlled at time of visit
transcript_quote: Right now? Sitting in my own chair with my cat and people being straight with me? About as good as it gets.
